VOLUME-PUBLICATIONS 

Poems  by  George  Meredith,    pp.  159. 

John  W.  Parker  &  Son.  [1881] 

"  To  Thomas  Love  Peacock,  Esq,,  this  volume  is  dedicated  with 
the  profound  admiration  and  affectionate  respect  of  his  sofirin-law. 
Weybridge,  May.  185 1." 

On  the  title-page  is  a  quotation  beginning,  "  Eos,  blest  goddess 
of  the  morning,"  from  R,  H,  Home's  "  Orion" 

All  the  poems  in  this  volume,  except  **  Should  thy  hoe  die" 
which  has  not  been  reprinted,  appear  in  Poems  {vol.  xxxi)  of  the 
Complete  Edition,  1898,  under  the  heading,  "Poems  written  in 
Early  Youth."  "  Love  in  the  Valley "  was  subsequently  re- 
written, and  in  the  later  form  has  been  frequently  reprinted.  It 
occurs  in  both  versions  in  Mr  Mosher's  book  of  selections, 
"  Modern  Love,  and  other  Poems,"  1898. 

Reviews. — Athenaum,  August  23,  185 1,  p.  895.  Critic, 
November  15,  185 1,  p.  539  (by  W.  M.  Rossetti).

"  New  Poets ").  Fraser*s  Magazine,  December, 
185 1,  p.  629  ([by  Charles  Kingsley]  in  an  article,  "  This 
Year's  Song-Crop ").  Guardian,  July  9,  1851, 
p.  496.  Leader,  July  5,  1851,  p.  635.  Spectator, 
July  5,  185 1,  p.  642.  See  also  Quarterly  Review, 
July,  1902.  "  A  Forgotten  Poet :  Geprge  Daiiey  ** 
[by  R.  A.  Streatfeild],  p.  178,  has  a  note  on  "  Love  in 
the  Valley."

first  twenty  lines  of  "By  the  Rosanna."  "  Modern  Love  '*  re- 
appeared in  1892  {after  an  interval  of  thirty  years  /)  with  "  The 
Sage  Enamoured  and  the  Honest  Lady,"  and  was  also  reprinted 
alone  in  1891,  and  with  selections  from  the  other  volumes  in  1898.

lected editions.  The  rest  of  this  volume  appears  in  vol.  iii  of 
the  Complete  Edition  of  1 896-1 898,  and  four  poems  {*'  Juggling 
Jerry,"  "The  Old  Chartist,"  "Martin's  Puzzle,"  and 
"  Marian  ")  in  vol.  ii.  of  the  New  Popular  Edition  (1898)  and

Uniform  Edition  of  the  Novels,  and  similarly  those 
for  1896-97  of  the  complete  edition.]

SYNOPTICAL  TABLE  OF  THE  POEMS 


The  numl>ers  stand  for  the  volumes,  as  follows  : 

I.  Poems,  185 1. 
II.  Modern  Love,  1862. 

III.  Poems  and  Lyrics  of  the  Joy  of  Earth,  1883. 

IV.  Ballads  and  Poems  of  Tragic  Life,  1887. 
V.  A  Reading  of  Earth,  1888. 

VI.  Poems,  1892. 
VII.  Modern  Love,  1892. 
VIII.  Jump-to-Glory  Jane,  1892. 
IX.  Select  Poems,  1897. 
X.  Odes  in  Contribution  to  the  Song  of  French  History,  1898. 
XI.  Poems  (2  vols.)  in  the  New  Popular  Editioii,  1898. 
XII.  Poems  (3  vols.)  in  the  Complete  £diti<Hi,  1898. 
XIII.  A  Reading  of  Life,  1901. 

Prefers  to  periodicals  of  the  dates  mentioned  ;  numbers  in  square 
brackets  are  dates,  and  refer  to  the  periodicals  under  those  years 
in  Section  I. 

The  scattered  fragments  of  verse  in  The  Shaving  of  Shagpai,  etc., 
are  not  included  in  this  table.

Commission.)

well-known  photograph  [side-iace]  which  has 
frequently  been  reproduced,  as  in  George  Meredith, 
by  Hannah  Lynch,  and  George  Meredith,  by 
Walter  Jerrold.)  • 

Punch,  December  19,  p.  300.     (A  small  caricature.) 

1893.  Portrait   [side-face],  by  G.  F.  Watts,  R.A.     (This 

portrait  was  afterwards  engraved  on  wood  by 
Biscombe  Gardner,  and  600  were  on  sale  at  the 
j)rice  of  one  guinea  each,  signed  by  both  artist 
and  engraver.  It  has  also  been  printed  in  photo- 
gravure by  Mr.  HoUyer,  and  is  on  sale  by  him  at 
los.  6d.     It  has  been  several  times  reproduced.) 

1894.  The  Tale  of  Chloe,  etc.,  frontispiece.     (Photograph 

[side- face]  by  J.  H.  Roller.)

by  Max  Beerbohm.)

THE  LIFE  OF  SIR  TOBIE  MATTHEW,  KNIQHT.  Bacon's 
Alter  Ego.  By  his  Kinsman  Arnold  Harris  Mathew  {dejure  Earl 
OF  Lanoaff,  of  Thomastown,  co.  Tipperary)  and  A  Calthrop. 
Numerous  Portraits  from  scarce  prints.     Demy  8yo.,  las.  ret. 

Thu  biography,  compiled  exclusively  from  original  and  chiefly  unexplored  docnments 
preserved  among  the  Domestic  and  State  Papers  of  the  Sixteenth  and  Seventeenth 
Centuries,  will  be  welcomed  by  students  of  those  periods,  and  by  Shakespearians  and 
Baconians  alike,  as  a  contribution  to  the  literature  in  which  they  are  mutually  interested 
as  the  opponents  or  advocates  of  the  Baconian  theory. 

WILLIAM  SHAKESPEARE :  A  Play  in  Three  Acts.  By  William 
T.  S AWARD.     Royal  i6mo.,  2s.  6d.  net. 

ORESTES:  a  Drama  in  Four  Acts.  By  William  T.  Saward. 
Crown  8vo.,  os.  6d.  net. 

THE  50NQ5  OF  51  Dl  HAMMO  :  Rendered  into  English  for  the 
first  time  by  R.  L.  N.  Johnston.  Edited,  with  a  Preface,  by  S.  L. 
Bensusan  (Member  of  the  Royal  Asiatic  Society,  and  Author  of 
"Morocco").  The  Verse-renderings  by  L.  Cranmer-Byng.  Facsimile 
Frontispiece  and  Cover  Design.     Royal  i6mo. ,  2S.  6d.  net. 

Sidi  Hammo  is  the  acknowledged  laureate  of  the  Berbers,  esteemed  by  countless 
thousands  as  the  ereatest  of  all  poets,  while  few  European  scholars  even  know  his  name. 
The  translation  of  the  work  of  this  famous  old  poet,  whose  songs  are  recited  daily  by  the 
tribes  of  Southern  Morocco,  has  been  made  by  Mr.  Johnston,  of  Mogador,  who  has  lived 
and  studied  among  the  Berbers  for  many  years,  and  have  been  taken  down  by  word  of 
mouth  in  the  villages  of  the  Atlas  Mountains,  and  are  presented  for  the  first  time  to  an 
English  audience. 

LONDON:   ELKIN  MATHEWS,  VIGO  STREET,  W.
